title: Rwanda Domestic Company Application for Incorporation (RDB Form RF-001)
description: Rwanda Development Board's (RDB) "RF-001 Domestic Company Application Form for Incorporation", used to register a domestic company under Law N° 007/2021 of 05/02/2021 governing companies. Opens Rwanda's Business Formation vertical (3 of 6, alongside DMV and Visa), continuing Rwanda's research thread from GOV-2569's scouting cycle, where this candidate was first identified and left as backlog. The specimen is a genuine, unauthenticated, directly downloadable 10-page PDF (18 numbered sections) hosted on RDB's own businessprocedures.rdb.rw domain -- the registry's own live Business Registration Guide site (matching the general online Business Registration process RDB's org.rdb.rw and business-registration pages describe as free and completed within hours). Unlike this registry's other two Rwanda schemas (rw/rra's Motor Vehicle Registration Form and rw/dgie's Visa Application), this specimen carries a real, position-anchored text layer (confirmed via pdfjs-dist) but, like rw/dgie, **0 AcroForm widgets** -- a flat, print-and-hand-fill specimen documenting the data RDB's registration process collects, rather than a fillable digital form in its own right. It models the shared RF-001 form's full printed breadth: the Registration-reason and Category (company-type) selectors; company Identification; the filing Applicant; the company's Head office address; an optional Chairman of the board; the Managing director; an optional Company employee/secretary; a bounded 2-slot Member of the board group; optional Auditor and Accountant blocks (each a Person-or-Organization alternative); a bounded 3-slot Business activities (RCPA code/name) group; company-level Capital information (a 5-subtype share table plus a Guarantee type/Amount table, each requiredWhen the selected Category implies shares or a guarantee respectively); a first Subscriber slot (Person-or-Organization, with its own share table); a first Guarantor slot (Person-or-Organization, with its own Guarantee type/Amount table, requiredWhen Category implies a guarantee); Employment; and the Amalgamation/Declaring-to-be-dormant/Dissolution sections, each requiredWhen the matching Registration-reason value. 256 `fields[]` entries in total and 19 `documents[]` entries (18 checklist attachments from Section 18 plus the printed certification statement); see VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, the AcroForm widget count, and every scoping/disclosure judgment call, including why several change-of-particulars-related attachments and the business-activities/board-member/subscriber/guarantor repeating groups are bounded rather than open-ended. This v1.0.0's conformance fixtures exercise the "new" (incorporation) pathway named in this schema's own title; GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Rwanda or the Rwanda Development Board.

source.documentRef: Form RF-001, "Domestic Company Application Form for Incorporation" (Rwanda Development Board), hosted on RDB's Business Procedures site (businessprocedures.rdb.rw); a genuine 10-page, position-anchored-text-layer but 0-AcroForm-widget PDF, 1,218,128 bytes.

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2585, authoring a candidate pre-scouted and confirmed strong in GOV-2569's research cycle but left unauthored. Independently re-fetched the source PDF directly via curl this cycle: HTTP 200, Content-Type application/pdf, 1,218,128 bytes, sha256 0bbcfa32fc17ea7d38365575bcadf72a7948c029bacd020e505757396700da8b -- matching the issue's own cited hash and size exactly, independently re-derived rather than trusted. pdfjs-dist (legacy build, v3) getAnnotations() across all 10 pages confirms 0 Widget annotations -- like rw/dgie/visa-application (and unlike rw/rra/vrf-e06-motor-vehicle-registration-form's genuine AcroForm/XFA), this is a flat, print-and-hand-fill specimen, notwithstanding its 1.2MB size (a real, position-anchored getTextContent() text layer throughout, not a scanned image -- confirmed by reading full per-page text and cross-checking item counts against the form's own 18 numbered sections). Every page's text was extracted with x/y coordinates (item.transform[4]/[5]) and read in visual (not content-stream) order, since this specimen's content stream interleaves its 2-column pages (e.g. page 10 prints Employment/Amalgamation/Dormant/Dissolution/Attachments sections 14-18 in one content-stream pass and the Attachments checkbox list plus Certification statement in another, requiring y-coordinate sorting to recover true reading order -- disclosed since naively concatenating getTextContent() items in stream order would have misread the section sequence). All 18 numbered sections were accounted for. This schema models 256 fields[] entries: the Registration-reason and Category (company-type) selectors (each a tick-box group modelled as a single closed enum, including Category's own printed asymmetry -- an 'Unlimited public' option with no 'Unlimited private' counterpart, a 'Limited by guarantee private' option with no public counterpart -- taken verbatim rather than symmetrized); company Identification; the filing Applicant (a full 17-field identity+address block, reused as a template across 6 person-slots: Applicant, Chairman, Managing director, Company secretary, and 2 Member-of-the-board slots); the Head office address; a bounded 2-slot Member of the board group (the base specimen prints exactly 2 slots before its own 'Add more pages as needed' note, matching this registry's established bounded-repeating-group convention, cf. ng/cac's 4 director slots); optional Auditor and Accountant blocks, each a Person-or-Organization alternative (Type selector plus person- or organization-only sub-fields gated by requiredWhen, sharing one address/contact sub-block per the source's own 'Person resident address /Organization registered office address' combined caption); a bounded 3-slot Business activities (RCPA code/name) group, flattened from the source's own unbounded ~17-blank-row dashed table; company-level Capital information (a 5-subtype share table -- Ordinary/Not-confer-voting/Special-limited-conditional-voting/Redeemable/Preferential-distribution -- plus a Guarantee type/Amount table), requiredWhen the selected Category implies shares or a guarantee respectively; a first Subscriber slot (Person-or-Organization, with its own share table, requiredWhen registrationType=new); a first Guarantor slot (Person-or-Organization, with its own Guarantee type/Amount table, requiredWhen Category implies a guarantee); Employment; and the Amalgamation/Declaring-to-be-dormant/Dissolution sections, each requiredWhen the matching Registration-reason value. Two judgment calls disclosed in the field descriptions themselves: (1) only each share table's Ordinary-share row is requiredWhen-gated when the table applies at all -- the other 4 printed share-subtype rows are additional, genuinely optional share classes, not co-required as a group (caught during this cycle's own conformance-fixture testing: an earlier draft gated all 5 subtypes identically, which a from-scratch positive fixture holding only ordinary shares correctly flagged as over-strict, then fixed before this schema was finalised); (2) the bounded business-activity Name fields are deliberately NOT requiredWhen-gated on their sibling RCPA-code field via notEquals empty-string, since an unused slot's RCPA-code field is absent (undefined), not an empty string, and gating on notEquals against an absent value would misfire -- this registry's own documented gotcha (cf. rw/dgie/visa-application's child1Gender precedent) -- disclosed in prose instead. 19 documents[] entries: 18 checklist attachments read from Section 18's two-column tick-box list (each requiredWhen-gated to its own uniquely-named registrationType/applicantPosition value where the mapping is unambiguous -- e.g. amalgamationProposal/amalgamationResolution to registrationType=amalgamation, powerOfAttorney to applicantPosition=power_of_attorney -- and left optional, undisclosed to any single requiredWhen condition, where a 'change_of_particulars' filing's several alternative sub-reasons (name change, articles alteration, branch opening, share issuance/division/reduction, limited<->unlimited conversion) would otherwise be wrongly co-required as a group) plus the printed Certification statement (attestation, required: true, quoted verbatim). Confirmed via live web search that Law N deg 007/2021 of 05/02/2021 governing companies (Official Gazette N deg 04 ter of 08/02/2021) is Rwanda's current companies statute, and that its three company-type categories (Limited by Shares, Limited by Guarantee, Limited by Shares and by Guarantee) correspond to this specimen's own printed Category options -- corroborating, not just citing, the source's currency. Conformance run: 2 valid mock scenarios (a Limited-by-shares-private new incorporation with a single individual subscriber and 0 employees; a Limited-by-guarantee-private new incorporation exercising the full officer roster -- Chairman, Company secretary, 2 Board members, an organization Auditor, a person Accountant, an organization Subscriber, and a person Guarantor -- plus the passport-ID-document branch), 0 errors each; 5 mutation-control fixtures (missing required field, invalid enum value, missing a category-conditional capital field, invalid date format, missing a required document), each raising exactly 1 error -- using a disposable validate_instance.mjs script (not committed) implementing this schema's own required/requiredWhen Condition grammar plus ad hoc type/validation checks, matching this registry's established from-scratch-conformance-checker convention. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, per-section field inventory, and every scoping/disclosure judgment call.

fields.name: registrationType
fields.label: Registration
fields.description: The single reason this filing is being made; the source prints these 8 options as one tick-box group ("Registration:"). This schema models the form's full printed breadth rather than narrowing to the incorporation-only pathway named in this schema's own title, since RDB uses one shared RF-001 specimen for all of new incorporation, subsequent particulars changes, dormancy, dissolution, amalgamation, correction, and court-ordered filings alike -- matching this registry's established full-breadth-over-narrowing precedent (cf. ng/nis Form C1's reasonForApplication). v1.0.0's conformance fixtures exercise the "new" (incorporation) pathway, consistent with this schema's title and the GOV-2585 assignment scope; the other 7 pathways' own downstream sections (15-17) are modelled with requiredWhen gates but not independently fixture-tested this cycle.
fields.sourceRef: "Registration:" — "New" / "Change of particulars" / "Declaring to be dormant" / "Dissolution" / "Amalgamation" / "Order of Correction" / "Cessation to be dormant" / "Court order"

fields.name: category
fields.label: Category (company type)
fields.description: The company's legal category, as the source's own 6-option tick-box group prints it verbatim -- including its own asymmetry (e.g. an "Unlimited public" option with no printed "Unlimited private" counterpart, and a guarantee-only "Limited by guarantee private" option with no printed public counterpart); this schema does not invent a symmetrical set the source itself does not offer.
fields.sourceRef: "Category:" — "Limited by shares private" / "Limited by shares public" / "Unlimited public" / "Limited by shares and guarantee private" / "Limited by shares and guarantee public" / "Limited by guarantee private"

fields.name: companyCode
fields.label: Company code
fields.description: Assigned by RDB once a company already exists in the registry; left blank on a New-incorporation filing and completed on a subsequent Change of particulars/Dormancy/Dissolution/Amalgamation/Correction/Court-order filing against an existing company.

fields.name: businessActivity1RcpaCode
fields.label: Business activity 1: RCPA code
fields.description: Section 10 prints a long, unbounded dashed table (~17 blank rows) for the company's business-activity codes/names under Rwanda's Common Products and Activities (RCPA) classification; flattened here to 3 numbered slots per this registry's established bounded-repeating-group convention. At least one business activity is required.
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 1, column "RCPA Code"

fields.name: businessActivity1Name
fields.label: Business activity 1: Name
fields.description: Expected to be filled in whenever businessActivity1RcpaCode is filled in, but not machine-gated with requiredWhen: businessActivity1RcpaCode is itself optional and absent (not empty-string) when unused, and comparing an absent sibling field against a sentinel value risks the misfire class this registry has already hit and fixed elsewhere (cf. rw/dgie/visa-application's child1Gender disclosure).
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 1, column "Name"

fields.name: businessActivity2RcpaCode
fields.label: Business activity 2: RCPA code
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 2, column "RCPA Code"

fields.name: businessActivity2Name
fields.label: Business activity 2: Name
fields.description: Expected to be filled in whenever businessActivity2RcpaCode is filled in, but not machine-gated with requiredWhen: businessActivity2RcpaCode is itself optional and absent (not empty-string) when unused, and comparing an absent sibling field against a sentinel value risks the misfire class this registry has already hit and fixed elsewhere (cf. rw/dgie/visa-application's child1Gender disclosure).
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 2, column "Name"

fields.name: businessActivity3RcpaCode
fields.label: Business activity 3: RCPA code
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 3, column "RCPA Code"

fields.name: businessActivity3Name
fields.label: Business activity 3: Name
fields.description: Expected to be filled in whenever businessActivity3RcpaCode is filled in, but not machine-gated with requiredWhen: businessActivity3RcpaCode is itself optional and absent (not empty-string) when unused, and comparing an absent sibling field against a sentinel value risks the misfire class this registry has already hit and fixed elsewhere (cf. rw/dgie/visa-application's child1Gender disclosure).
fields.sourceRef: Section 10: Business activities — RCPA Code / Name table, row 3, column "Name"

documents.label: Resolution to change the name
documents.handling: one of several alternative sub-reasons a "change_of_particulars" filing can carry (see registrationType); this schema does not capture which specific particular is changing as its own selector, so this and the following 6 change-of-particulars-related attachments are left optional rather than all requiredWhen-gated to registrationType alone (which would wrongly force every one of them whenever any single particular changes)
documents.sourceRef: Section 18: Attachments (checklist) — "Resolution to change the name"

documents.label: Certification that all information is complete and correct
documents.statement: I hereby certify that the information given to the best of my knowledge is true, complete and all signatures in the attachments are made by the persons stated. I also understand that any false declaration may lead to prosecution.
documents.sourceRef: Page 10, "Certification" — "I hereby certify that the information given to the best of my knowledge is true, complete and all signatures in the attachments are made by the persons stated. I also understand that any false declaration may lead to prosecution."
